title: PC Randomly Restarts
cardText: Diagnose computers that restart unexpectedly during normal use, gaming, startup, or idle periods.
Check overheating, power supply stability, memory, drivers, Windows errors, and hardware faults.
Separate software issues from power and hardware failures.
text: This guide is for computers that unexpectedly restart without warning.
Determine whether the restart occurs under load, while idle, or during startup.
Check temperatures, event logs, drivers, memory, and power stability.
Escalate hardware faults when software troubleshooting does not resolve the issue.

checkRestartPattern.title: Identify when the restart occurs
body: Determine whether the restart happens during gaming, heavy workloads, startup, or random idle use.
The timing often provides clues about the underlying cause.

checkTemperatures.title: Check system temperatures
body: Monitor CPU and GPU temperatures while the system is under load.
Overheating can trigger automatic restarts to prevent hardware damage.
Listen for unusually loud fans or signs of thermal throttling.

resolveCoolingIssue.title: Cooling issue identified
body: The system is overheating.
Clean dust from vents and fans, verify fan operation, and improve airflow.
Retest after cooling issues are resolved.

checkEventViewer.title: Check Event Viewer
body: Review System logs around the restart time.
Look for Kernel-Power events, driver crashes, or hardware-related errors.
Event logs can help identify the source of unexpected restarts.

investigateLoggedIssue.title: Investigate logged issue
body: Review the reported error or warning.
Research the specific driver, service, or hardware component mentioned.
Apply any recommended fix before continuing.

checkPowerSupply.title: Check power supply stability
body: Unexpected restarts are commonly caused by failing power supplies.
Watch for restarts during gaming, rendering, or other high-power workloads.
If available, test with a known-good PSU.

replacePsu.title: Power supply fault likely
body: The power supply appears unstable.
Replace or test with a known-good power supply.
Retest system stability afterwards.

checkMemory.title: Check system memory
body: Faulty RAM can cause sudden restarts without warning.
Run a memory diagnostic or memory test utility.
If multiple RAM sticks are installed, test them individually if necessary.

replaceMemory.title: Memory fault identified
body: Memory errors were detected.
Replace the faulty RAM and retest system stability.

checkDrivers.title: Check drivers
body: Review recently updated drivers.
Graphics, chipset, storage, and network drivers are common causes of instability.
Update or roll back drivers if the issue started recently.

repairDriverIssue.title: Driver issue identified
body: A driver appears responsible for the restarts.
Update, roll back, or reinstall the affected driver.
Monitor stability after making changes.

checkRecentChanges.title: Check recent software or hardware changes
body: Determine whether the problem started after installing software, hardware, updates, or drivers.
Recent changes often provide a clear starting point for troubleshooting.

undoRecentChange.title: Undo recent change
body: Remove or reverse the recent change if appropriate.
Retest system stability afterwards.

checkDiskHealth.title: Check storage health
body: Failing SSDs or HDDs can cause crashes and unexpected restarts.
Review SMART data and storage health information.
Watch for disk errors in Event Viewer.

replaceStorage.title: Storage fault identified
body: The storage device appears unreliable.
Back up important data immediately.
Replace the drive and restore or reinstall the operating system.

checkBiosFirmware.title: Check BIOS and firmware
body: Outdated firmware can cause instability.
Check for BIOS, motherboard, and device firmware updates.
Apply updates according to manufacturer instructions.

updateFirmware.title: Update firmware
body: Install available firmware updates.
Retest the computer after updating.
Firmware updates can resolve stability problems.

hardwareEscalation.title: Further hardware diagnostics required
body: Temperature, power, memory, storage, drivers, and firmware have been checked.
Possible causes include motherboard faults, CPU instability, GPU faults, or intermittent hardware failures.
Escalate for advanced hardware diagnostics.
